response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. To determine how many bottles she needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of days in a leap year, which is 366 days. Since she uses shampoo every other day, she uses it 366 ÷ 2 = 183 times. Each bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo, so we need to find how many bottles are required to cover 183 uses. The calculation is 183 × (1/4) = 45.75 ounces of shampoo needed. Since she can't buy a fraction of a bottle, she will need 46 bottles. This means she will need [46] bottles to get through a leap year.

[46] bottles will provide enough shampoo for her to wash her hair every other day during a leap year. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so 46 bottles provide 644 ounces of shampoo. Since she uses 1/4 ounce every other day, 644 ounces will last 644 ÷ (1/4) = 2576 days, which is more than enough for a leap year.
